message.reasoning:
The user gave a long prompt describing the runtime. The task at top says: "Gather the exact evidence needed before editing schema definitions so fixture, task, evidence, trace, score, and run artifacts stay versioned and aligned."

We have seen several actions already: expansions, hydrations, etc. The active code windows show crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs with some symbols. The frontier suggests expanding file symbols. We need to choose next action to gather exact evidence needed before editing schema definitions. Likely we need to find schema version constants definitions. Possibly need to search for definitions of schema version symbols like RUN_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION, CONTEXT_OBJECT_SCHEMA_VERSION, etc. Could use graph.find to locate symbol definitions? Or use session.expand_file to view file? Or maybe use graph.path? The frontier suggests expand_file on [F1] relation=- direction=- candidates=17 priority=112 to expand file symbols for artifacts.rs.

Thus we can perform a tool call session.expand_file with target selector maybe "F1"? The target is a node id: maybe "F1" corresponds to the file node. Indeed, the frontier says expand_file [F1] relation=- direction=- candidates=17. So we need to call session.expand_file with payload specifying target selector? The tool_name is session.expand_file@v1. Payload includes target selector and optional depth, limit, relation filters, priority threshold, traversal budgets. We need to provide target: maybe "F1". According to earlier instructions: Use exact short ids as provided in the prompt when referring to graph nodes. So target is "F1". So call session.expand_file with payload: {"target":"F1"} maybe also include depth? But we can just pass target.

Thus we need to output JSON with kind: tool_call, tool_call: {tool_name:"session.expand_file@v1",payload:{ "target":"F1" } }, acquired_fact_ids: [], readiness_state: "not_ready" perhaps. Or maybe we need to set readiness_state based on status? The format says: {{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}. So when kind is tool_call, we must include tool_call and maybe acquired_fact_ids empty, readiness_state something. Probably "not_ready". The assistant_message can be brief status like "Expanding file symbols". So output JSON accordingly.

Thus produce:

{
  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"Expanding file symbols for artifacts.rs",
  "tool_call":{"tool_name":"session.expand_file@v1","payload":{"target":"F1"}},
  "acquired_fact_ids":[],
  "readiness_state":"not_ready"
}

Make sure no extra fields. Ensure JSON only, no markdown.
